idea.hook: How tightly packed can an alien solar system be?
facts: Our Solar System: Mercury, our innermost planet, orbits the Sun at ~58 million km (0.39 AU).
The TRAPPIST-1 system has 7 Earth-sized planets, all orbiting a cool red dwarf star.
The outermost planet, TRAPPIST-1h, orbits its star at just ~9 million km (0.06 AU).
idea.payoff: The ENTIRE TRAPPIST-1 system, with 7 planets, could easily fit inside Mercury's orbit!
idea.title: TRAPPIST-1: SO TIGHT! 🤯🪐

scenes.text: How TIGHTLY PACKED can an alien solar system be?
scenes.visual: A vast, dark space background with distant stars. A subtle question mark icon might appear briefly.

scenes.text: Our Solar System: Mercury's orbit is ~58 Million km (0.39 AU).
scenes.visual: The Sun appears in the center. An animated orbit ring for Mercury fades in, then Mercury itself appears on the orbit.

scenes.text: The TRAPPIST-1 system has 7 Earth-sized planets around a Red Dwarf.
scenes.visual: Transition to a small, cool red dwarf star. Faint, close-in orbital paths for 7 planets appear around it.

scenes.text: TRAPPIST-1h (outermost) orbits at just ~9 Million km (0.06 AU).
scenes.visual: Zoom in slightly on the TRAPPIST-1 system. The outermost orbit (TRAPPIST-1h) is highlighted, with the planet visible.

scenes.text: The ENTIRE TRAPPIST-1 system fits INSIDE Mercury's orbit!
scenes.visual: Mercury's orbit (from Scene 2) reappears as a large, transparent ring. Inside it, the entire TRAPPIST-1 system (star and all 7 orbits) is shown, visibly tiny, floating within Mercury's orbit. An arrow points from Mercury's orbit to the TRAPPIST-1 system.
